name: Pogy (SS-266)
events: 17 Feb 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales, USN) departed New Orleans, Louisiana for the Panama Canal Zone.
21 Feb 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales, USN) arrived at Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone from New Orleans, Louisiana for a training period.
5 Apr 1943 With her trials and initial training completed USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) arrived at Pearl Harbor.
15 Apr 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the eastern coast of Honshu, Japan.
1 May 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) torpedoed and sank the Japanese auxiliary gunboat Keishin Maru (1434 GRT) off Iwaki, Japan in position 37°04'N, 141°06'E.
2 May 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) sank a Japanese sampan with gunfire east of Honshu, Japan in position 37°11'N, 143°40'E.
9 May 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese troop transport Uyo Maru (6376 GRT) off Iwaki, Japan in position 37°05'N, 141°06'E.
11 May 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) sank a Japanese sampan with gunfire east of Honshu, Japan in position 37°52'N, 143°12'E.
26 May 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Tainan Maru (1989 GRT) off Shioyazaki, Honshu, Japan in position 37°03'N, 141°09'E.
5 Jun 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
26 Jun 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) departed from Midway for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Caroline Islands area.
31 Jul 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) torpedoed and sank the Japanese aircraft transport Mogamigawa Maru (7509 GRT, built 1934) north-west of Truk in position 11°08'N, 153°18'E.
16 Aug 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) ended her 2nd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
9 Sep 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Palau's.
30 Sep 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army transport Maebashi Maru (7005 GRT) about 300 nautical miles east of Palau in position 06°01'N, 139°08'E.
26 Oct 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. G.H. Wales) ended her 3rd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
25 Nov 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 4th war patrol. Once again she was ordered to patrol off the Palau's.
7 Dec 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese collier Soyo Maru (6081 GRT) north of Truk in position 14°03'N, 152°20'E.

13 Dec 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese army cargo ship Fukkai Maru (3829 GRT) off the Palau Islands in position 07°06'N, 134°30'E. Pogy is damaged by depth charges and forced to terminate her patrol.
22 Dec 1943 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) ended her 4th war patrol at Midway.
5 Feb 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) departed from Midway for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Formosa.

10 Feb 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese destroyer Minekaze (1350 tons, offsite link) and the Japanese merchant cargo ship Malta Maru (5499 GRT) about 85 nautical miles north-north-east of Formosa in position 23°12'N, 121°30'E.

20 Feb 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese cable layer Nanyo Maru (3614 GRT) about 100 nautical miles east of Formosa in position 24°12'N, 123°20'E, and the Japanese merchant cargo ship Taijin Maru (5154 GRT) north-east of Formosa in position 24°14'N, 123°17'E.

23 Feb 1944 While attacking a Japanese convoy some 60 miles west of Okinawa USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Horei Maru (5588 GRT) in position 26°20'N, 126°11'E and torpedoed and damaged the Japanese merchant tanker Teikon Maru (5113 GRT) in position 26°22'N, 126°16'E.
24 Feb 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) bombards a phosphate plant on Rasa Island.
8 Mar 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) ended her very successful 5th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
7 Apr 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol south-east of Japan.

28 Apr 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese submarine I-183 (1630 tons, offsite link) about 30 nautical miles south of Cape Ashizuri, Japan in position 32°07'N, 133°03'E.
5 May 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Shirane Maru (2825 GRT) off Shionomisaki, Honshu, Japan in position 33°27'N, 135°32'E.
13 May 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Awe Maru (4532 GRT) off Suruga Bay, Honshu, Japan in position 34°31'N, 138°33'E.
16 May 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) sank a Japanese sampan with gunfire south of Honshu, Japan in position 33°56'N, 139°50'E.
20 May 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) sank a Japanese sampan with gunfire south of Honshu, Japan in position 31°21'N, 140°03'E.
29 May 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Metcalf) ended her 6th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
1 Jun 1944 USS Pogy departed from Pearl Harbor bound for the Hunters Point Navy Yard for a major overhaul.
8 Jun 1944 USS Pogy arrived at the Hunters Point Navy Yard.
17 Sep 1944 With her overhaul completed USS Pogy departed from the US west coast bound for Pearl Harbor.

2 Oct 1944 USS Halibut (T/Cdr. I.J. Galantin, USN), USS Pogy (T/Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr., USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Allen (Lt. J.A. Rowe, USNR), USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Manlove (Lt. E.P. Foster, Jr., USNR), USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Widgeon (Lt. C.W. Engler, USN).

3 Oct 1944 USS Tuna (T/Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr., USN), USS Pogy (T/Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr., USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Allen (Lt. J.A. Rowe, USNR), USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Fleming (Lt. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR), USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Widgeon (Lt. C.W. Engler, USN).
13 Oct 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr.) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 7th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Ryukyu Islands.
2 Dec 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr.) ended her 7th war patrol at Midway.
27 Dec 1944 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr.) departed from Midway for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bonin Islands.

24 Jan 1945 At 0100 I/24, instructions were received by USS Burrfish (T/Lt.Cdr. M.H. Lytle, USN) to make rendezvous with USS Pogy (T/Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr., USN) and USS Ronquil (T/Lt.Cdr. R.B. Lander, USN).

Rendezvous was effected at 1700 I/24 to the north of Tori Shima, Bonin Islands in position 31°00'N, 140°20'E. A 50 knot gale blowing and mountainous sea however prevented information being passed by line between the submarines so at 2030 I/24, USS Burrfish which was group commander, passed information to the other two submarines through VHF. A search was then commenced by all three submarines, procceding on course 090° at 10 knots. The submarines were placed 20 miles apart. (1)
11 Feb 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr.) ended her 8th war patrol at Midway.
12 Mar 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) departed from Midway for her 9th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol south of Honshu, Japan.
19 Apr 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) is mistakenly attacked an American aircraft as Pogy approaches a Japanese convoy south-east of Honshu, Japan in position 32°59'N, 139°58'E.
15 May 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) ended her 9th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
2 Jul 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 10th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Sea of Japan.

27 Jul 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Chikuzen Maru (2448 GRT) south-west of Kyogasaki about 90 nautical miles north of Tottori, Honshu, Japan in position 37°00'N, 134°02'E.

5 Aug 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Kotohirasan Maru (2220 GRT) in the Japan Sea about 30 nautical miles west of Akita, Honshu, Japan in position 39°52'N, 138°52'E.
21 Aug 1945 USS Pogy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Bowers) ended her 10th war patrol at Midway.
